hook.text: What if particles aren't random? Pilot wave theory says they follow hidden waves deterministically.

buildup.text: Louis de Broglie proposed in 1927 that a real wave guides each particle along a definite path.

discovery.text: David Bohm revived the idea in 1952. In his version, particles always have positions—they're just hidden.

twist.text: The theory reproduces all quantum predictions but requires instant nonlocal connections—spooky action.

climax.text: Most physicists prefer Copenhagen, but pilot wave theory proves determinism isn't ruled out.

punchline.text: Maybe God doesn't play dice. Maybe we just can't see the waves.

quiz.question: What does pilot wave theory propose about quantum particles?
options: They follow deterministic paths guided by hidden waves
They split into multiple copies at each interaction
